Showers Ending; Cooler

Last Updated Thu May 07 at 11:02AM

A few lingering light showers this morning taper off by early afternoon and become more limited to the western edge of the Appalachians. We'll have some breaks in the clouds this afternoon, but clearing is delayed until late tonight. Friday and Saturday have pleasant spring weather with cool mornings and mild afternoons. A weakening front moves through Saturday afternoon with little impact other than passing clouds. Sunday is also dry with increasing clouds later in the day as the next front approaches from the northwest.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest

Voting in the 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest ended Sunday. We will announce the winners by the end of this week. Many thanks to everyone who submitted photos and voted in the contest. It's going to be another awesome calendar!

Get the free RWC Mobile App...: Apple and Android

Forecast Discussion

A trough expands from the Mississippi Valley into the eastern U.S. through the weekend before gradually weakening next week.

The surface reflection of this change is a cold front just to our southeast this morning. It presses east and south and will be off the Atlantic Coast and in the Gulf by early Friday. We have a few lingering light showers this morning. Those showers taper off early this afternoon and become more limited to the western escarpment of the Appalachians. Clouds decrease over a dry Thursday night.

High pressure takes over from west to east Friday. Friday and Saturday have pleasant spring weather with cool mornings and mild afternoons. A weakening front moves into the area late Saturday afternoon with little impact on our weather.

Sunday is also dry as the next weak front approaches from the northwest. Showers are possible late Sunday night into Monday morning before ending Monday afternoon.

The next high moves across the Southern Appalachians Tuesday with cool temperatures and dry weather.

Yet another front approaches from the northwest Wednesday, bringing showers back into play by late in the day and Wednesday night.

Expect another big pattern change, this time toward warmer weather, beginning late next week.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.
